examples: In recent decades, environmental threats have expanded beyond regional boundaries to have global reach and, most hauntingly, are multiplying at a dizzying rate.
The car drove past at a dizzying speed.
The variety of plastic objects at risk is dizzying: early radios, avant-garde sculptures, celluloid animation stills from Disney films, the first artificial heart.
The experience is almost dizzying, as the birds flit about like Tinker Bells of the forest.
